structure.text:
Subject: NCM-P-2025-11-25 FHFA Announces Conforming Loan Limits for 2026
From: Nova Capital Markets <capitalmarkets@novahomeloans.com>
Date: 2025-11-25 12:39:36-07:00



ANNOUNCEMENT SUMMARY

The Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) today announced the conforming loan limits (CLLs) for conventional mortgages to be acquired by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac (the Enterprises) in 2026. In most of the United States, the 2026 CLL value for one-unit properties will be $832,750, an increase of $26,250 from $806,500 in 2025.  

 

One-Unit Limit

Two-Unit Limit

Three-Unit Limit

Four-Unit Limit

$832,750

$1,066,250

$1,288,800

$1,601,750

 

IMPORTANT NOTES

*	Counties with a limit greater than the National Baseline ($832,750) are “High Balance” counties, with the higher limit allowed using High Balance programs. The attached excel spreadsheet contains all 2026 limits by county. 
*	The national conforming limit has been updated in Polly for standard (non-High Balance) counties and is available for locks effective immediately.
*	High balance counties will become available following Polly’s official product release.  Prior to the product release, the new High Balance county limit can be referenced in the attached excel document.
*	A follow-up announcement will be issued in the coming days advising when High Balance county limits have been updated in Polly.

 

ATTACHMENTS

*	County Loan Limit List Excel Spreadsheet
*	FHFA Announcement
*	Marketing Materials

** For NOVA Home Loans Internal Distribution Only **

Contact Us:

Scenario Desk <mailto:Scenariodesk@novahomeloans.com>         Lock Desk <mailto:lock.desk@novahomeloans.com>         Product Department <mailto:productdept@novahomeloans.com>
